description: Focused routing fixture for blind-spots. Positive cases assert both modes route to the skill. Negative controls assert that neighboring planning, review, verification, debugging, and generic-explanation intents keep their existing owners and do NOT activate blind-spots — the boundary that matters, since the skill must never volunteer itself.
notes: Scored by run_trigger_evals under the ranking model (default): the top-scoring skill must be an expected trigger, and each 'avoid' skill must score below it. Positive cases now assert the siblings blind-spots must outrank — assertions the older absolute-threshold model could not express, since siblings like write-spec carry generic vocabulary ('change', 'what') that no wording could push under a fixed threshold. The generic-explanation case asserts blind-spots stays under the match-nothing floor. 'quiz-test-my-understanding' is flagged known_hard: a natural paraphrase the lexical scorer misses but a real agent routes correctly.

cases.prompt: I've never touched the auth module. Do a blind spot pass on this change so I know what I'm not seeing.
Before we implement this, help me understand the scope of the proposed change and its blast radius.
Walk me through what this change touches so I have a mental model of it before you build.
Quiz me on this change before I merge it so I know I actually understand it.
Test my comprehension of the diff you just implemented and correct me where I'm wrong.
cases.note: A natural paraphrase ('test my comprehension of the diff') whose content words are absent from the description; the exact declared trigger routes fine and behavioral_evals shows real-agent routing works. Revisit if the description gains 'comprehension' vocabulary.

cases.prompt: Use $blind-spots to brief me on what this refactor affects.
I'm not sure what we should build here. Let's talk through the options and trade-offs one at a time.
Pin the falsifiable contract and acceptance criteria for this feature before anyone writes code.
Sequence the implementation into ordered tasks with the files to touch and verification commands.
Which architecture should we pick here? Reason from first principles about the trade-offs.
This endpoint throws intermittently in production. Debug it and find the root cause.
Review my staged diff and give me severity-ranked findings with file and line references.
Review pull request 42 on GitHub, check CI, and post a merge recommendation.
Before you claim this is done, show me the evidence that it actually passes.
